content:
# HNSW Index Management — Decisions (March 2026)

## Demotion (Warm→Cold)
Lazy orphaning: delete Redb UUID↔HNSW mappings during GC cycle. Single rebuild at end of cycle if any demotions occurred. Not one rebuild per entry.

## Promotion (Cold→Warm)
Synchronous hnsw_rs insert() — O(log n), sub-millisecond. Recreate Redb mappings. No async needed.

## Rebuild Triggers
- End of GC cycle (if demotions occurred)
- After delete_scope
- After manual corvia_gc_run
- When orphan ratio > 20%
- Blue-green pattern: build new index in background, atomic swap under mutex

## Cold Entry Search
Brute-force cosine scan over Redb-stored embeddings. Only when include_cold=true or Hot+Warm results below relevance threshold. Acceptable up to ~10K cold entries (<10ms).

## PostgresStore
Partial HNSW indexes with WHERE clause. Tier changes handled automatically by PostgreSQL. No manual rebuild.

## Deferred
Secondary cold HNSW index — only needed above 50K cold entries.
